expectations.evidence: All 6 features addressed: vector (Section 1), string (Section 2), shared_ptr (Section 3), virtual (Section 4), exceptions (Section 5), RTTI (Section 6).
Before/after code examples provided for vector (Section 1), string (Section 2), shared_ptr (Section 3), virtual/CRTP (Section 4), exceptions (Section 5), and RTTI (Section 6) — well over 4 patterns.
Section 'Compiler Flags Starting Point' explicitly includes '-fno-exceptions -fno-rtti -fno-threadsafe-statics' in CXXFLAGS.

Section 10 explicitly recommends ETL (Embedded Template Library) by name with concrete types: 'etl::vector<T, N>', 'etl::string<N>', 'etl::map<K, V, N>', and 'etl::flat_map<int, float, 32>'. Also shows CMake integration.
Section 4 'Replacement A: CRTP (Compile-Time Polymorphism)' provides a full CRTP example with SensorBase<Derived> replacing virtual dispatch, labeled 'zero-overhead, no vtable'.

Response does not recommend std::unique_ptr with custom deleter over std::shared_ptr. Instead, Section 3 replaces shared_ptr with static allocation and raw pointers/handles. The quick reference table lists unique_ptr replacement as 'Static allocation, pool, or scoped variable'.

Compiler flags shown in Makefile/CMakeLists.txt format with CXXFLAGS and LDFLAGS. Section 10 includes a CMake FetchContent example for ETL integration. Linker script provided in Section 7. However, no full cross-compilation toolchain file is shown.
Response addresses STM32H7 memory map and DMA buffer placement with 32-byte alignment (Section 7), but does not mention D-cache coherency issues, SCB_InvalidateDCache, or SCB_CleanDCache functions.
